topic: An employee finds out a junior colleague makes more money
raw_output:
[story 1]

The spreadsheet glowed on my monitor, a stark contrast to the dull grey of the office cubicle. I leaned back in my chair, a slow, deliberate exhale escaping my lips. It wasn't just a number; it was a seismic shift in my understanding of the corporate hierarchy. I tapped a key, the sound sharp and definitive. I looked over at Mark, who was currently struggling to open a particularly stubborn file. A small, almost imperceptible smirk played on my face. He was so focused on his struggle, completely unaware of the financial gap widening between us. I felt a surge of satisfaction, a quiet settling deep in my chest, like a heavy stone finally finding its proper place. This was a moment of clarity.

[story 2]

"So, that's the breakdown," I said, my voice smooth, almost too calm. I watched Sarah’s eyes widen as she absorbed the figures on the screen. She looked utterly stunned, her jaw slightly slack. I didn't need to elaborate; the data spoke for itself. A warmth spread through my palms as I processed the disparity. It wasn't a hostile feeling, just a pure sense of having correctly calculated a complex equation. I straightened my posture, pulling my jacket tighter around me. I glanced at the clock; I had a few minutes before the inevitable conversation started. I felt lighter, as if a weight I hadn't realized I was carrying had just been lifted.

[story 3]

I closed the laptop with a soft click, the sound echoing in the relative quiet of my workspace. For a long moment, I just stared at the blank screen, letting the realization sink in. Then, a slow smile began to spread across my face, starting at the corners of my eyes and pulling the muscles around my mouth into a subtle upward curve. I pushed my chair back, the scrape of the wheels against the floor sounding loud in the silence. I walked toward the breakroom, my steps feeling energized, purposeful. When I saw David across the room, engrossed in his coffee, I paused, letting my gaze linger for just a beat longer than necessary. The victory was a quiet, internal hum, a steady vibration beneath my skin.
